description: Front brake caliper pistons may corrode and seize, reducing stopping power on 2019-2021 Touring models (Road King, Street Glide, Road Glide, Electra Glide) equipped with Brembo front calipers. Corrosion accelerated by road-salt exposure; symptoms include spongy lever, uneven pad wear, and pulsing under hard braking.
remedy: Dealer replaces front brake caliper assembly (both sides) with updated-spec pistons and seals. Free under recall. Approximately 1.8 hours labor per side.

description: Hydraulic clutch master cylinder on M8-powered Softail models may lose pressure due to internal seal swelling from non-OEM DOT-5 silicone fluid contamination. Affects Fat Boy, Heritage, Deluxe, Slim, Low Rider, Street Bob, Breakout. Symptom: clutch lever goes soft or to the bar, no disengagement.
remedy: Dealer replaces master cylinder, bleeds system with OEM DOT-4 fluid. Approximately 0.9 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: BMS firmware fault may halt charging mid-cycle on 2020 LiveWire electric motorcycles. All VINs affected. Battery may display 'charge error' and bike won't accept DC fast charge. Cold-weather (<10C) cycles particularly susceptible.
remedy: Dealer reflashes BMS firmware to revision v1.0.4.2. No hardware change required. Approximately 0.5 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Stator coil may short to ground due to insulation breakdown in specific batch manufactured Sep 2018 - Mar 2019. Symptoms: battery not charging, voltage drops below 13V at idle, MIL may illuminate. VIN-range scoped.
remedy: Dealer replaces stator assembly and checks regulator output. Approximately 3.0 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Shift arm return spring may break on 2020-2022 CBR1000RR-R Fireblade and SP variants. Broken spring results in gear indicator malfunction and inability to upshift cleanly past 4th gear. Affects bikes with quickshifter option.
remedy: Dealer replaces shift arm assembly with updated-spec return spring (PN 24611-MKF-D40). Approximately 1.2 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Side-stand switch may fail intermittently on CRF1100L Africa Twin, causing false 'stand down' signals that stall the engine when leaning into corners. Particularly affects DCT variants after extended off-road use (mud ingress into switch body).
remedy: Dealer replaces side-stand switch with sealed-body updated part. Approximately 0.6 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Airbag inflator on 2018-2020 Gold Wing GL1800 may rupture during deployment due to moisture-compromised propellant (Takata-legacy inflator batch). Deployed airbag fragments may injure rider. Affects all trims.
remedy: Dealer replaces airbag module with updated sealed-inflator unit. Approximately 1.5 hours labor. Free under recall. Do not operate until repair is complete.

description: Front brake master cylinder piston seal may extrude under sustained high-speed braking (>130 km/h) on 2021-2022 MT-09 and MT-09 SP. Results in brake drag after release, reducing mileage and potentially causing rotor warp.
remedy: Dealer replaces master cylinder piston/seal kit. Approximately 0.8 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Crankshaft position sensor wiring harness may chafe against engine case on 2020-2021 YZF-R1 and R1M. Chafe can short the signal wire, causing intermittent MIL, stalling under load, and no-start conditions.
remedy: Dealer installs protective loom sleeve and reroutes harness away from case. Approximately 1.0 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Fuel hose supplying fuel rail on 2018-2022 Ninja 400 and Z400 may develop pinhole leaks from ethanol-induced swelling. Leak may cause engine bay fire under prolonged high-load riding.
remedy: Dealer replaces main fuel hose with ethanol-resistant updated hose (PN 51044-0744). Approximately 0.8 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Fuel pump on the newly-introduced 2022 KLR650 (fuel-injected revival) may fail prematurely due to internal filter clogging from tank sediment. Symptom: loss of power above 80% throttle, lean-misfire codes, stalling.
remedy: Dealer replaces fuel pump assembly with updated filter spec. Approximately 1.5 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Hydraulic cam chain tensioner on 2017-2020 GSX-R1000 and GSX-R1000R may fail to maintain pressure after cold soak, causing start-up rattle and, in severe cases, chain skip that contacts valves.
remedy: Dealer replaces cam chain tensioner with updated high-volume unit. Approximately 2.3 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: ECU firmware on 2021-2022 Hayabusa (3rd-gen) may mis-interpret throttle-by-wire signals during aggressive closed-loop corrections, causing momentary power loss between 8000 and 10000 RPM. Safety concern during passing maneuvers.
remedy: Dealer reflashes ECU to firmware revision B.2. Approximately 0.4 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Side-stand pivot bolt on 2021-2022 SV650 and SV650X Cafe may loosen over time due to undersized thread-locker coverage at assembly. Loose bolt can drop the stand while riding, causing loss of control.
remedy: Dealer removes pivot bolt, applies updated-spec thread-locker, re-torques to 40 N·m. Approximately 0.3 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Front fork internal damper rod on 2020-2021 890 Duke and 890 Duke R may seize due to undersized oil passage. Results in binding compression stroke, sudden hydraulic lock, and potential rider ejection during hard braking.
remedy: Dealer rebuilds both fork legs with updated-spec damper cartridges (WP Apex PN 48.6.2.1-2021). Approximately 3.8 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Engine oil cooler hose on 2020-2022 1290 Super Duke R and Super Duke R Evo may rupture where it passes near exhaust header. Insufficient heat-shield clearance. Rupture causes sudden oil loss and engine seizure.
remedy: Dealer installs additional heat shield and replaces oil cooler hose with braided-stainless updated part. Approximately 2.0 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Fuel tank strap clamp bolt on 2019-2021 500 EXC-F may back out during aggressive off-road use, allowing tank to shift and contact handlebar at full lock. Risk of fuel line disconnect.
remedy: Dealer replaces clamp with locking-spec clamp and updated bolt. Approximately 0.4 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Swingarm pivot bolt on 2020-2022 Panigale V4, V4 S, and V4 R may be under-torqued from factory, leading to progressive clearance in the pivot bearings. Symptoms: chatter under acceleration, uneven tire wear, weave above 200 km/h.
remedy: Dealer checks pivot bolt torque and, if below spec, replaces bolt and torques to 120 N·m. Approximately 1.4 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Electronic steering damper on 2019-2021 Multistrada 1260, V2, and V4 may lock in full-resistance mode after cold startup, preventing slow-speed steering and causing low-speed drops.
remedy: Dealer reflashes damper controller firmware and verifies initialization cycle. Approximately 0.6 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Final drive chain on 2021-2022 Monster 937 and Monster 937 Plus may develop tight links due to insufficient factory lubrication, causing chain-snatch on throttle roll-on and accelerated sprocket wear.
remedy: Dealer replaces final drive chain and front sprocket, applies updated-spec chain lube. Approximately 1.0 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: ABS pump on 2019-2021 R1250GS and R1250GS Adventure may fail to pressurize after extended off-road use, disabling ABS and partially disabling rear-brake function. Water ingress past updated O-ring.
remedy: Dealer replaces ABS pump O-ring seal and repressurizes system. Approximately 1.6 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Slipper clutch assembly on 2020-2022 S1000RR, S1000R, and S1000XR may develop excessive free play over 15000 km, causing clutch slip at high throttle angles in 4th-6th gears. Symptom: engine revs climb without corresponding acceleration.
remedy: Dealer replaces clutch pack with updated-spec friction plates. Approximately 2.2 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Steering head bearing on 2018-2020 K1600GT, K1600GTL, and K1600B may develop a detent notch from insufficient preload, causing dead-spot steering feel at slow speed and weave above 180 km/h.
remedy: Dealer replaces upper and lower bearing with updated tapered-roller units and re-preloads per updated spec. Approximately 3.2 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Fuel tank breather hose on 2020-2021 Tiger 900 GT, Rally, and Rally Pro may kink where it enters the frame tunnel, causing tank pressurization and fuel-pump starvation under hard cornering.
remedy: Dealer reroutes breather hose with updated clip kit (PN T2404500). Approximately 0.5 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Regulator/rectifier mounting bracket on 2022 Speed 400 and Scrambler 400X may resonate and crack above 8000 RPM, causing charging-system failure and eventual non-start. Newly-introduced model.
remedy: Dealer installs updated damped-mount bracket and inspects regulator for heat damage. Approximately 0.7 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Hydraulic clutch slave cylinder on 2018-2020 Speed Triple 1050 RS and Speed Triple 1200 RS may leak at the seal-to-body joint, causing clutch drag and eventual no-disengage. Often mistaken for clutch pack wear.
remedy: Dealer replaces slave cylinder with updated one-piece body unit. Approximately 1.1 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: ECU firmware on 2021-2022 RSV4 Factory and RSV4 1100 may cause false over-temperature shutdown in ambient >32C during track use. Engine cuts to 50% power mid-session.
remedy: Dealer reflashes ECU to firmware revision 4.3.1 with corrected temperature-compensation table. Approximately 0.5 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Fork seals on 2019-2021 Tuono V4 1100 Factory may fail prematurely due to contaminated assembly lube. Oil weeping onto brake caliper reduces braking efficiency.
remedy: Dealer replaces fork seals and dust wipers; if contamination found, replaces damper oil. Approximately 1.8 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: OEM skid plate mounting bolts on 2022 Tuareg 660 may back out under off-road vibration, allowing skid plate to contact exhaust and cause heat damage to engine case paint.
remedy: Dealer applies thread-locker to all four mounting bolts and inspects for contact. Approximately 0.2 hours labor. Free under recall.

description: Fuel line quick-disconnect on 2017-2020 Sportster Iron 883 and Forty-Eight may separate partially under tank-flex during hard acceleration, causing intermittent fuel starvation or in rare cases fuel leak at the connector.
remedy: Dealer replaces fuel line with updated locking-tab connector (PN 62400103). Approximately 1.0 hours labor. Free under recall.
